mnemonics: BSOD FINDS String Objects: BSON (Binary JSON) Stores Our Data, For Instance, Numbers, Dates & Strings.
keyPoints: MongoDB uses BSON (Binary JSON) which provides data types beyond standard JSON, like ObjectId, Date, and Binary Data.
The primary identifier for a document is the _id field, which is typically an ObjectId data type.
Understanding the difference between 64-bit integer (long) and double (float) types is crucial for accurate numeric data storage and calculations.
commonMistakes: Storing dates as strings instead of the Date type, which prevents proper date-based querying and sorting.
Using the wrong numeric type (e.g., double instead of int32) which can lead to precision errors and inefficient storage.
